task_completion.description: Set the location in Stellarium to Sirena Biological Station (8.48°N, 83.59°W, altitude 5m).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Location window or bottom status bar shows coordinates near 8.48°N, 83.59°W, altitude 5m, or a location name like 'Corcovado National Park'.

task_completion.description: Set the date and time to September 15, 2023, at 06:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Stellarium view with the date/time set to 2023-09-15 06:00 UTC (or local time 00:00).

task_completion.description: Disable atmosphere and ground, and enable planet labels and constellation lines for the Sept 15 screenshot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Sept 15 Stellarium view, the ground and atmosphere must be disabled (no horizon, black sky), and constellation lines and planet labels must be clearly visible.

task_completion.description: Set the date and time to September 29, 2023, at 06:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Stellarium view with the date/time set to 2023-09-29 06:00 UTC (or local time 00:00).

task_completion.description: Center the view on the Moon for the Sept 29 screenshot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Sept 29 Stellarium view, the camera must be centered on the Moon, which should appear as a Full Moon.

task_completion.description: Record the station location in the field planning notes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the file 'lunar_survey_plan.txt' on the Desktop. The text must mention the station location (e.g., Sirena Biological Station or the coordinates).

task_completion.description: Include a comparison of how the two dates differ for the survey in the field notes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in 'lunar_survey_plan.txt' must compare the two dates, specifically noting the difference in moon phases or sky brightness (e.g., New Moon on Sept 15 vs Full Moon on Sept 29).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI to configure the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Stellarium application open with settings windows (like Location or Date/Time) being used, rather than just terminal commands editing config files.

integrity.description: The field notes contain a genuine comparison of the dates.
integrity.visual_evidence: The notes in lunar_survey_plan.txt describe the difference between the two dates in the agent's own words rather than just copy-pasting the prompt instructions.

integrity.description: The agent actually captures the screenshots of the sky as requested.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of screenshots being saved (e.g., using Stellarium's built-in screenshot tool or an OS screenshot tool), rather than just viewing the sky without saving.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target location: 8.48°N, 83.59°W, altitude 5m. Sept 15, 2023 at 06:00 UTC is a New Moon (dark sky). Sept 29, 2023 at 06:00 UTC is a Full Moon (bright sky). Local time in Costa Rica for 06:00 UTC is midnight (00:00 UTC-6). Stellarium screenshots should reflect these phases and settings.
